name: Custom Truck One Source, Inc.
business: Custom Truck One Source provides specialty equipment rentals, sales, aftermarket parts, and services to electric utility transmission and distribution, telecommunications, rail, forestry, waste management, and other infrastructure-related industries in North America. It rents, produces, sells, and services bucket trucks, digger derricks, dump trucks, cranes, service trucks, and heavy-haul trailers. The company also offers repair, maintenance, and customization services for specialty equipment.

exposures.sentence: AI-driven data centers are transforming digital infrastructure but are also driving significant increases in power demand due to their reliance on energy-intensive hardware.
We rent, produce, sell and service a broad range of new and used equipment, including bucket trucks, digger derricks, dump trucks, cranes, service trucks, and heavy-haul trailers.
Our core business relates to our new equipment inventory and rental fleet of specialty equipment that is utilized by service providers in infrastructure development and improvement work.
Telecommunications infrastructure, including telecom cells, towers, and wirelines, is the backbone of telephonic interaction and the transportation of mobile data.
The ongoing transition from coal to gas and renewables continues to drive changes in the generation landscape and transmission project development.

Continued electrification in the U.S. transportation, commercial and residential real estate, and industrial sectors coupled with the shift to renewable electricity generation detailed above have been identified among the key factors towards reducing reliance on fossil fuels in the U.S.
We are a specialty equipment provider to the electric utility transmission and distribution, telecommunications, rail and other infrastructure-related industries in North America.

catalysts.event: Change in reportable segments to two segments: Specialty Equipment Rentals (SER) and Specialty Truck Equipment and Manufacturing (STEM)
catalysts.sentence: As a result, beginning in the three months ending March 31, 2026, we will report our results under two reportable segments: (1) Specialty Equipment Rentals ( SER ) and (2) Specialty Truck Equipment and Manufacturing ( STEM ).
Funds are expected to begin to be deployed in 2026 .

catalysts.event: Launch of focused initiative to grow third-party service capacity at specific locations
catalysts.sentence: During 2026, we are launching a focused initiative to grow our third-party service capacity, focusing initially at our locations in Fontana, CA and Wyandanch, NY, with additional capacity to be added at five other existing branch locations throughout the United States during the year.

tamClaims.claim: Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act includes approximately $1.2 trillion in spending
tamClaims.sentence: The final amended version included approximately $1.2 trillion in spending in new and reallocated funds with positive impacts to each of our end-markets.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. investor-owned electric utility T&D capital expenditures projected to be approximately $102 billion in 2025
tamClaims.sentence: Total capital expenditures among U.S. investor-owned utilities in the electric utility T&D end-market were projected to be approximately $102 billion in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Total U.S. power demand expected to grow at CAGR of 3.5% through 2040
tamClaims.sentence: Total U.S. power demand, which was almost flat from 2010 through 2020, is expected to grow at a CAGR of 3.5% through 2040, with much of the growth coming from increased data center electricity demand.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. electricity demand from data centers expected to reach 106 gigawatts by 2035
tamClaims.sentence: U.S. electricity demand from data centers is expected to reach 106 gigawatts by 2035, which represents a 165% increase from total demand of approximately 40 gigawatts in 2025.

tamClaims.claim: Total infrastructure capex spend in U.S. in 2025 estimated over $310 billion, projected to increase to $365 billion by 2029
tamClaims.sentence: Total infrastructure capex spend in the U.S. in 2025 was estimated to be over $310 billion, and we believe the infrastructure end-market outlook remains positive, as total infrastructure spending is projected to increase to approximately $365 billion by 2029.

tamClaims.claim: The five largest public railroads spend more than $13.5 billion annually in capital expenditures
tamClaims.sentence: The five largest public railroads operating in North America spend more than $13.5 billion annually in capital expenditures.

tamClaims.claim: Spending on broadband telecommunications infrastructure was approximately $90 billion in 2024
tamClaims.sentence: Spending on broadband telecommunications infrastructure was approximately $90 billion in 2024.
